obligation_text: Training, validation and testing data sets shall be subject to data governance and management practices appropriate for the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system. Those practices shall concern: relevant design choices; data collection processes and the origin of data; relevant data-preparation processing operations such as annotation, labelling, cleaning, updating, enrichment and aggregation; the formulation of assumptions about the information the data are supposed to measure and represent; an assessment of the availability, quantity and suitability of the data sets that are needed; examination for possible biases likely to affect health and safety or fundamental rights; appropriate measures to detect, prevent and mitigate possible biases; the identification of relevant data gaps or shortcomings.
scoring_text: Skill in AI training data governance, including design choice documentation, data provenance tracking, data preparation operations management, assumption formulation and documentation, data adequacy assessment, bias detection and mitigation, and data gap identification for high-risk AI systems.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in AI training data governance, including design choice documentation, data provenance tracking, data preparation operations management, assumption formulation and documentation, data adequacy assessment, bias detection and mitigation, and data gap identification for high-risk AI systems.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing AI governance frameworks that address the full AI lifecycle from procurement or build through development, testing, deployment, monitoring, and retirement.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 10(2) (obligation) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-S-002 is the strongest match (STS: 0.622, cosine: 0.736). Secondary: AG-S-008 (AG, cosine: 0.731), AG-S-001 (AG, cosine: 0.729).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.2/10 (raw 0.622). Shared vocabulary: [ai, governance, skill]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [adequacy, assessment, assumption, bias, choice, data] vs WIDAI terms [address, build, deployment, designing, development, frameworks] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.855 (P=0.846, R=0.866). R exceeds P by 0.020 — the WIDAI KSA's 23 words are well-captured by the competency implication's 36 words, but the competency implication contains broader scope.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.6%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.736 > STS 0.622 (gap +0.114). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, governance, skill] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 10.9%. Shared tokens: [ai, governance, skill]. EU AI Act-only: [adequacy, assessment, assumption, bias, choice, data]. WIDAI-only: [address, build, deployment, designing, development, frameworks].
